Teacher Talk 1 POV.2 (level 2.0) I can make inferences about what is implied in the text based on contextual clues

outcomes.title: Complexity of an Issue (11-12.RI3.B)
outcomes.description:
The student will:

  • Synthesize information from two or more texts about similar ideas/topics to articulate the complexity of the issue, using specific examples to support their analysis

outcomes.title: Follow a Writing Process: Voice (11-12.W.2.A)
outcomes.description: Follow a writing process to produce clear and coherent writing to ensure the voice of the writing is appropriate to the task, purpose, and audience.

outcomes.display_name: Relate Themes/Central Ideas to Human Nature
outcomes.title: Relate Themes/Central Ideas to Human Nature (11-12.RL.1.D/11-12.RI.1.D)
outcomes.description: Relate themes/central ideas within a text to human nature and the world, providing specific examples to support their analysis.

outcomes.title: Complexity of a Theme (11-12.RL.3.B)
outcomes.description: Synthesize ideas from two or more texts about similar themes or topics to articulate the complexity of the theme, using specific examples to support their analysis.

outcomes.title: Author's Point of View (11-12.RL.1.D/11-12.RI.1.D)
outcomes.description: Analyze a text in which the author's point of view is not obvious, identifying clues or strategies the author uses to convey perspective indirectly.

outcomes.display_name: Point of View: Directly Stated vs. Implied
outcomes.title: Point of View: Directly Stated vs. Implied (11-12.RL.2.B/11-12.RI.2.B)
outcomes.description: Distinguish what is directly stated and what is implied in the text, using specific examples to support their analysis.

outcomes.title: Research to Solve a Problem (11-12.W.1.A)
outcomes.description: Conduct research to solve a problem; narrow or broaden the scope of research when appropriate to ensure the problem is thoroughly investigated; gather multiple relevant, credible sources, print and digital; integrate information using a standard citation system.

outcomes.title: Claims & Counterclaims (11-12.W.2.A)
outcomes.description: Develop argumentative writing by acknowledging counterclaims; establishing relationships among claims, counterclaims, and supporting evidence.

outcomes.title: Writing; Organization & Content (11-12.W.3.A.a)
outcomes.description: Achieve the writer's purpose through choices in organization and content.

outcomes.title: Revise & Edit: Transitions (11-12.W.3.A.d)
outcomes.description: Review, revise, and edit writing with consideration for the task, purpose, and audience to use a variety of appropriate transitions to clarify relationships, connect ideas or claims, and signal time shifts.

outcomes.title: Draw Conclusions and Infer (11-12.RI.1A / 11-12.RL.1A)
outcomes.description: Draw conclusions and infer by citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support analysis of what the text says explicitly.

outcomes.title: Analyze Text (RL.1.A/RI.1.A)
outcomes.description: Analyze text by citing relevant and thorough textual evidence to support inferences drawn from the text, including where the text leaves matters uncertain.

outcomes.title: Analyze How Themes Develop (11-12.RL1.D/11-12.RI.1.D)
outcomes.description: Analyze how themes/central ideas develop throughout the text, providing specific examples to support their analysis.

outcomes.title: Writing: Enhance Reader's Understanding (11-12.W.3.A.a)
outcomes.description: Enhance the reader's understanding and experience with the text through choices in organization and content.
